name: codex-later
description: Stop-hook with two end-of-turn jobs, both via a Codex consult. (1) WORK FRONTIER: when the turn left open non-[USER] items, apply: 1. Finish the requested objective. 2. Classify discovered work. 3. Immediately drain the discovered-work frontier when it is safe, relevant, and tool-executable. 4. Stop only when the frontier contains no worthwhile safe work, or only user-owned/irreversible decisions remain. Execution loop: objective -> required fixes -> verification -> discovered frontier -> drain -> verify -> stop. (2) ADR / SPEC ALIGNMENT: when the turn changed code AND the project has a registered ADR (docs/adr/) or grounded-docs specification index, review the change against it per @docscheck and, on divergence, ALIGN it -- Codex reviews + proposes the fix (apply it), with do:distinguished-engineer (implementation) / do:test-engineer (tests) as fallbacks -- or, when the user sets ASK_CODEX_ALLOW_EDITS=1 (opt-in, off by default), Codex applies the fix directly. Blocks once; self-gates on the do.manifest; recursion-safe; decline-respecting; the alignment branch self-suppresses where no ADR/spec is registered; FAIL-OPEN throughout. Replaces the old local codex-on-question hooks, which fired on any '?'. DISCLOSURE: the consult it asks for sends the (secret-scrubbed) frontier items / changed-code context to an EXTERNAL LLM via the codex skill; the hook itself emits only a directive and egresses nothing.

disclosure.target: codex CLI / reasoner via the codex skill (gpt-5.5 / OpenAI)
disclosure.dataSent: the open non-[USER] frontier items and turn context, ONLY when Claude runs the codex skill the directive asks for; the hook itself sends nothing. The codex skill secret-scrubs before egress (fail-closed).
disclosure.execution: advisory by default -- the hook never runs codex; Claude invokes the codex skill as a normal, visible tool call. Codex runs READ-ONLY unless the user opts in with ASK_CODEX_ALLOW_EDITS=1, which lets Codex apply the alignment fix directly (codex.sh runs workspace-write -- an external LLM writing to the repo, off by default).
